Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint;
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF KEVIN LINDSETH'S Motion For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint is Granted. No opposition filed. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC.'S (Renewed) Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings is denied. Moving party has not shown a basis to grant this renewed motion to dismiss or stay proceedings. Moving party has not shown that the balace of private and public interest factors shows justice would be served if the litigation proceeded in an alternative forum. Trial is scheduled to begin in San Francisco on September 16, 2013. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: OSCAR RAMIREZ, ESQ. AND ALLEN B. FELAHY, ESQ., 562.499.2121, FOR PLAINTIFF KEVIN LINDSETH.
GREGORY A. ZBYLUT, ESQ., 909.276.4TAX, FOR DEFENDANTS BOLIGALA RAJU & HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS BOLIGALA RAJU, HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the first cause of action for specific performance and sustained without leave to amend, as to the second cause of action. Plaintiff has pled facts sufficient to allege a cause of action for specific performance. Defendant asserts issues of fact, which are not the proper subject of a demurrer. Plaintiff stipulates to the demurrer of the second cause of action as to Defendant Raju. Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: allen felahy, esq. and oscar ramirez, esq., felahy law group, 562.499.2121, for plaintiff kevin lindseth.

gregory zbylut, esq., zbylut law office, 909.276.4TAX, and steven sorell, esq., 626.792.8600, for defendants boligala raju and high quality organics, inc.

RULING: no argument. the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs BOLIGALA RAJU, HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Off calendar in light of the court's ruling on Defendants' demurrer. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Mtn To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: allen felahy, esq. and oscar ramirez, esq., felahy law group, 562.499.2121, for plaintiff kevin lindseth.

gregory zbylut, esq., zbylut law office, 909.276.4TAX, and steven sorell, esq., 626.792.8600, for defendants boligala raju and high quality organics, inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS BOLIGALA RAJU, HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC.'s motion to Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings is Denied. The forum selection clause is permissive and Defendants have not shown either that California is a seriously inconvenient forum or that the interests of justice require that this case be in Nevada. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: allen felahy, esq. and oscar ramirez, esq., felahy law group, 562.499.2121, for plaintiff kevin lindseth.

gregory zbylut, esq., zbylut law office, 909.276.4TAX, and steven sorell, esq., 626.792.8600, for defendants boligala raju and high quality organics, inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: defendants boligala raju and high quality organics, inc.'s demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action; and defendant's demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action, and plaintiffs are encouraged to plead on additional cause for breach of contract. as to the first cause of action, plaintiff has not pled any prospective controversy that requires declaratory relief. as to the third cause of action, plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege why that he lacks an adequate legal remedy.
Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
